rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WENTZ FAMILY LIVING TRUST DATED MARCH 14, 2011
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Defendant'S Responses Without Objection To Petitioner'S Form Interrogatories (Set One) And Request For Monetary Sanctions Attorneys Fees And Costs Against Respondent Brooke Marie Wentz And Her Counsel
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Stephen M. Murphy. Before the court is Petitioner's Motion to Compel Respondent's Responses Without Objections to Petitioner's Form Interrogatories (Set One) and Request for Monetary Sanctions/Attorneys Fees' and Costs Against Respondent Brooke Marie Wentz and Her Counsel, pursuant to Cal. Code of Civil Proc. sec. 2030.290(b). On August 6, 2024, Respondent filed a Responsive Declaration of Attorney John E. Fasesky. Although this filing was late, the Court has considered it. The Motion is GRANTED for the reasons set forth below. A party propounding interrogatories may move for an order compelling responses if the party to whom interrogatories are directed fails to serve timely responses. (Cal. Code of Civil Proc. sec. 2030.290(b).) On February 20, 2024, Petitioner Scott Seebach Wentz propounded Form Interrogatories (Set One) on Respondent Brooke Marie Wentz (See Declaration of Ingrid M. Evans In Support of Motion to Compel Responses Without Objections to Petitioner Scott Seebach Wentz's Form Interrogatories (Set One) and Request for Monetary Sanctions/Attorneys Fees' and Costs Against Respondent Brooke Marie Wentz and Her Counsel, Exh. A.). Respondent Brooke Marie Wentz failed to respond timely to Petitioner's discovery requests. Pursuant to Cal. Code of Civil Proc. sec. 2030.290(a), Respondent's objections are waived for failure to timely serve responses. Since Respondent Brooke Marie Wentz has served responses to Form Interrogatories (Set One), Petitioner's Motion is moot except for the issue of sanctions. Petitioner's request that Respondent Brooke Marie Wentz and her counsel be jointly and severally ordered to pay monetary sanctions is GRANTED pursuant to Cal. Code of Civil Proc. sec. 2023.010. A party who fails to respond or to submit to an authorized method of discovery is subject to sanctions for misuse of the discovery process. (Cal. Code Civil Proc. sec. 2023.010(d).) Respondent failed to timely respond to Petitioner's Form Interrogatories (Set One) and is therefore subject to sanctions. The Court does not find Respondent acted with substantial justification in opposing this motion to compel or that other circumstances make the imposition of sanctions unjust. Respondent Brooke Marie Wentz and her counsel John E. Fasesky of Kato, Feder & Suzuki, LLP, jointly and severally, are ordered to pay Petitioner Scott Wentz five hundred and sixty dollars ($560.00), within 15 days of service of Notice of Entry of this Order. This sanction award shall be paid in addition to any sanctions awarded as to Petitioner's Motion to Compel Defendant's Responses to Special Interrogatories (Set One), and Petitioner's Motion to Compel Defendant's Responses to Requests for Production (Set One). The moving party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WENTZ FAMILY LIVING TRUST DATED MARCH 14, 2011
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Respondents Responses Without Objection To Requests For Production (Set One) And Request For Monetary Sanctions Attorneys Fees And Costs Against Respondent Brooke Marie Wentz And Her Counsel
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Stephen M. Murphy. Before the court is Petitioner's Motion to Compel Respondent's Responses Without Objections to Petitioner's Requests for Production (Set One) and Request for Monetary Sanctions/Attorneys Fees' and Costs Against Respondent Brooke Marie Wentz and Her Counsel, pursuant to Cal. Code of Civil Proc. sec. 2031.300(b). On August 6, 2024, Respondent filed a Responsive Declaration of Attorney John E. Fasesky. Although this filing was late, the Court has considered it. The Motion is GRANTED for the reasons set forth below. A party making demands for production may move for an order compelling responses if the party to whom the demands are directed fails to serve timely responses. (Cal. Code of Civil Proc. sec. 2031.300(b).) On February 20, 2024, Petitioner Scott Seebach Wentz propounded Requests for Production (Set One) on Respondent Brooke Marie Wentz (See Declaration of Ingrid M. Evans In Support of Motion to Compel Responses Without Objections to Petitioner Scott Seebach Wentz's Requests for Production (Set One) and Request for Monetary Sanctions/Attorneys Fees' and Costs Against Respondent Brooke Marie Wentz and Her Counsel, Exh. A.). Respondent Brooke Marie Wentz failed to respond timely to Petitioner's discovery requests. Pursuant to Cal. Code of Civil Proc. sec. 2031.300(a), Respondent's objections are waived for failure to timely serve responses. Since Respondent Brooke Marie Wentz served responses to Requests for Production (Set One), Petitioner's Motion is moot except as to Petitioner's Request for Production No. 51 and as to the issue of sanctions. Respondent Brooke Marie Wentz shall serve a response to Request for Production (Set One) No. 51 within 15 days of service of a Notice of Entry of Order. The response must comply with Cal. Code of Civil Proc. sec. 2031.210 et seq.: In the first paragraph of the response immediately below the title of the case, there shall appear the identity of the responding party, the set number, and the identity of the propounding party. Each answer in the response shall bear the same identifying number and be in the same sequence as the corresponding item or category in the demand. Respondent shall respond in writing, under oath, and separately to each demand. If Respondent Brooke Marie Wentz fails to obey this Order compelling an answer, the court may make further orders that are just, including the imposition of an issue sanction, an evidence sanction, or a terminating sanction under Cal. Code of Civil Procedure sec. 2023.010 et seq. In lieu of or in addition to that sanction, the court may impose a monetary sanction under Cal. Code of Civil Proc. sec. 2023.010 et seq. (Cal. Code of Civil Proc. sec. 2031.320(c).) Petitioner's request that Respondent Brooke Marie Wentz and her counsel be jointly and severally ordered to pay monetary sanctions is GRANTED pursuant to Cal. Code of Civil Proc. sec. 2023.010. A party who fails to respond or to submit to an authorized method of discovery is subject to sanctions for misuse of the discovery process. (Cal. Code Civil Proc. sec. 2023.010(d).) Respondent failed to timely respond to Petitioner's Requests for Production (Set One) and is therefore subject to sanctions. The Court does not find Respondent acted with substantial justification in opposing this motion to compel or that other circumstances make the imposition of sanctions unjust.
